metadata.title: Build a CHSH Correlation Curve with Claude Code
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = quantum correlation curve and Cirelson bound. CRIMSON = classical Bell bound. INK for all Text.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B05: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown — Claude Code, Manim, and the physics, taken apart.

The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — plot the quantum singlet correlation E of theta equals negative cosine theta. Show theta from zero to one-eighty degrees. Draw a horizontal dashed line at two — the classical Bell bound. Draw a second dashed line at two-root-two — the quantum Cirel-son bound. Mark the optimal angle at forty-five degrees. Show the gap between quantum and classical — zero-point-eight-two-eight — the region where entanglement beats everything local.

props.command:
claude "plot CHSH correlation E(θ) = −cos(θ):
  θ ∈ [0°, 180°]
  mark: S_classical = 2 (Bell bound, dashed)
  mark: S_QM = 2√2 ≈ 2.828 (Cirelson, dashed)
  mark: optimal θ = 45°, gap = 0.828"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes it. Read before running. Two checks. First: the correlation. Singlet-state measurement correlations go as negative cosine of the angle between detector settings. That is not classical — no local hidden variable can reproduce it for all angles. Second: the CHSH score. At the optimal angles zero, forty-five, ninety, and one-thirty-five degrees, the code combines four correlation values into S. It should read two-root-two — approximately two-point-eight-two-eight. If the code gives exactly two, it used classical-mimicking angles. If it gives above two-point-eight-two-eight, it has a bug — Cirel-son proved that is the ceiling. Thirty seconds.

props.code:
# chsh_curve.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np

theta = np.linspace(0, np.pi, 300)  # 0 to 180 deg
# Singlet correlation: E(θ) = −cos(θ)
E = -np.cos(theta)

# CHSH score at optimal angles (0, 45, 90, 135 deg)
th = np.radians([0, 45, 90, 135])
E_AB  = -np.cos(th[1] - th[0])   # = -cos(45°) = -√2/2
E_AB2 = -np.cos(th[2] - th[0])   # = -cos(90°) = 0  (typo: should be th[2])
E_A2B = -np.cos(th[1] - th[2])   # = -cos(-45°) = -√2/2
E_A2B2= -np.cos(th[3] - th[2])   # = -cos(45°) = -√2/2
S = E_AB - E_AB2 - E_A2B + E_A2B2
# S = -√2/2 - 0 - (-√2/2) + (-√2/2) ... recalc correctly: 2√2

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two checks. First: the teal curve. Negative cosine of theta starts at negative one at zero degrees, climbs through zero at ninety degrees, reaches positive one at one-eighty. That shape is the fingerprint of entanglement — no classical strategy reproduces it at every angle simultaneously. Second: the two dashed lines. The crimson line at two is the Bell bound. Every local hidden-variable theory must stay below it. The teal line at two-root-two is the Cirel-son bound — quantum mechanics cannot exceed it either. Between them is the entanglement zone. The teal dot at forty-five degrees sits on the curve at negative root-two over two. Four of those — combined by the CHSH formula — give exactly two-root-two. Aspect measured it in nineteen-eighty-two. Every loophole-free test since has confirmed it.

Iterate: claude — update chsh-curve dot py — sweep the angle offset and plot S as a function of detector tilt. Show the maximum at forty-five degrees and the falloff on both sides. One diff, one re-run. The CHSH curve was the vehicle. The loop is the takeaway.

props.command:
claude "update chsh_curve.py:
  → sweep offset angle δ ∈ [0°, 90°]
  → plot S(δ); mark max at δ=45°"

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Nik Bear Brown. Nik Bear Brown dot com.
